description: Electrical quality and inspection knowledge for industrial construction — torque verification, megger testing, hi-pot procedures, ground resistance testing, conduit inspection, cable tray inspection, motor testing, transformer testing, and visual inspection checklists. What the QC inspector and foreman check before sign-off.

torque_verification.why: Loose electrical connections cause arcing, fire, and equipment failure. Torque verification is the single most important QC check in electrical work.

14_10_AWG.note: Small wire — easy to overtorque and break conductor

1_0_to_4_0.note: Use ft-lb torque wrench for these sizes

torque_verification.critical_rule: ALWAYS use manufacturer torque spec on the equipment label. General guidelines are fallback only. Every breaker, lug, bus connection, and terminal has a specific torque value.
torque_verification.procedure: Use calibrated torque wrench. Apply torque smoothly — no impact wrench. After torquing, mark with torque-seal paint or verification marker. Re-torque after 24 hours on critical connections (thermal cycling loosens initial torque).

bus_connections.switchgear_bus: Typically 30-40 ft-lbs for 1/2 inch hardware. Belleville washers where specified — they maintain spring tension.
bus_connections.MCC_bus_splice: Per manufacturer — typically 20-30 ft-lbs. Sequence: center bolt first, then alternate outward.
bus_connections.transformer_bushings: Follow manufacturer spec exactly. Over-torque cracks porcelain bushing = catastrophic failure.

torque_verification.common_mistake: Using an impact wrench on electrical connections. Impact wrenches cannot be calibrated for torque. Always use hand torque wrench.

procedure: Disconnect cable from all equipment at both ends
Disconnect surge protectors, VFDs, capacitors, electronic controls
Verify cable is de-energized and grounded
Remove ground connection from cable under test
Connect megger: LINE to conductor under test, EARTH to ground/shield
Apply test voltage for 1 minute — record reading at 1 minute
For PI test: continue to 10 minutes — record at 1 and 10 minutes
After test: ground conductor for at least 4x the test duration to discharge
Test each conductor to ground, then each conductor to each other conductor
cable_testing.temperature_correction: Insulation resistance halves for every 10C rise in temperature. Correct all readings to 40C (104F) for comparison. Use IEEE 43 temperature correction table.
cable_testing.record_keeping: Log date, cable ID, test voltage, temperature, humidity, 1-minute reading, 10-minute reading (if PI), PI ratio. Compare to previous tests — trending downward indicates deteriorating insulation.

motor_testing.preparation: Disconnect motor leads from starter/VFD. Test at motor terminal box.

tests.phase_to_ground: Each phase (T1, T2, T3) to motor frame. All three should be similar.
tests.phase_to_phase: T1-T2, T2-T3, T1-T3. All three should be within 5% of each other. Low reading between two phases = shorted turns.

motor_testing.acceptance: Minimum 5 megohms per IEEE 43. New motor should be 100+ megohms. Below 5 megohms = do not energize.
motor_testing.practical_note: If motor has been sitting idle in humid conditions, readings may be low due to moisture absorption. Megger readings below 5 megohms on idle motor — try space heater or running motor uncoupled for several hours, then re-test.

DC_hi_pot.purpose: Overvoltage withstand test — proves insulation can handle voltage stress above normal operating conditions.
DC_hi_pot.test_voltage_new_cable: For 5kV cable: 25.2kV DC (80% of factory test). For 15kV cable: 52kV DC.
DC_hi_pot.test_voltage_maintenance: 60% of new cable test voltage for maintenance tests.
DC_hi_pot.hold_time: 5 minutes for acceptance, 15 minutes for maintenance per IEEE 400.
DC_hi_pot.leakage_current: Record leakage at each voltage step and during hold. Increasing leakage during hold = insulation problem.
DC_hi_pot.CAUTION: DC hi-pot can damage XLPE insulation (water treeing). VLF (Very Low Frequency) AC testing preferred for XLPE cable.

VLF_testing.purpose: AC withstand test at 0.01-0.1 Hz. Better for XLPE cable than DC.
VLF_testing.test_voltage: 3x rated voltage (phase to ground) for 60 minutes per IEEE 400.2.
VLF_testing.advantage: Finds defects that DC misses in XLPE. Does not promote water tree growth like DC testing.
VLF_testing.equipment: VLF test set — specialized equipment. Brands: HV Diagnostics, BAUR, Megger, b2 electronic.

fall_of_potential_method.description: THE standard method for measuring ground rod or ground grid resistance.
fall_of_potential_method.equipment: Ground resistance tester (Megger DET series, Fluke 1625, AEMC)
setup: Disconnect ground rod from system (IMPORTANT — test isolated rod only)
Drive current stake (C2) at least 5x the diagonal of the ground system away from the electrode under test
Drive potential stake (P2) at 62% of the distance from ground rod to current stake
Connect instrument leads: C1 to ground rod, P2 to potential stake, C2 to current stake
Take reading — this is the ground resistance
fall_of_potential_method.verification: Move P2 stake to 52% and 72% of distance. All three readings should be within 10% — if not, move C2 further away and repeat.
fall_of_potential_method.acceptance: NEC requires maximum 25 ohms for single rod. Most industrial specs require 5 ohms or less for ground grid. Substations typically require 1 ohm or less.

soil_resistivity.method: Wenner 4-pin method — measures soil resistivity to design ground grid.
soil_resistivity.high_resistivity: Sandy or rocky soil = hard to get low resistance. May need longer rods, more rods, ground enhancement material (bentonite, chemical ground rods).
soil_resistivity.low_resistivity: Clay soil, high moisture = easy to get low resistance.

visual_checklist: Correct conduit type for the area classification and environment
Support spacing per NEC (10 ft max for EMT and RMC, within 3 ft of box)
All fittings tight — wrench-tight for threaded, compression for EMT in wet locations
Maximum 360 degrees of bends between pull points
Bushings on all conduit terminations (protect wire from sharp edges)
Expansion fittings on PVC runs over 25 feet exposed
Sealing fittings within 18 inches of explosion-proof equipment (Chico compound filled)
Conduit bodies accessible (not buried behind walls)
No conduit crushing, kinking, or deformation at bends
Drain fittings at low points in outdoor conduit runs
Fire-stop at all penetrations through fire-rated walls and floors
conduit_inspection.threading_inspection: Minimum 5 full threads engaged in fitting. Thread compound (anti-seize) on threads. For explosion-proof: verify thread engagement by measuring gap at fitting — max 1/4 inch gap per UL.
conduit_inspection.practical_note: Walk every conduit run before wire pull. Finding a problem after pulling wire means pulling it all back out. Inspect first, pull second.

checklist: Correct tray type and size per design
Support spacing per NEC 392 and engineering (typically 8-12 ft)
Splice plates with bonding jumper at every section joint
Tray grounding per NEC 392.60 — bonding conductor sized per largest cable
Cable ties or straps securing cables (not damaging jacket)
Covers installed where specified (above walkways, near heat sources)
Tray not used as walkway or storage (common abuse — enforce it)
Labels on cables identifying circuit number

turns_ratio.purpose: Verify winding ratio matches nameplate. Detects shorted turns, wrong tap setting.
turns_ratio.method: Apply low voltage to one winding, measure induced voltage on other winding. Ratio should match nameplate within 0.5%.

tests.HV_to_LV: High voltage winding to low voltage winding — tests inter-winding insulation

insulation_resistance.test_voltage: 2500V DC for distribution transformers. 5000V DC for substation transformers.
insulation_resistance.acceptance: Compare to manufacturer data. Trending is more important than absolute value — 25% decrease from previous test = investigate.

winding_resistance.purpose: Detects loose connections, broken strands, bad tap changer contacts.
winding_resistance.method: DC resistance measurement of each winding. All phases should be within 2% of each other.
winding_resistance.equipment: Micro-ohmmeter or winding resistance test set (Megger MTO, AEMC)

tests.dielectric_strength: D877 or D1816. Minimum 30kV for in-service oil. Below 26kV = recondition or replace oil.
tests.dissolved_gas_analysis: DGA detects internal faults by analyzing gases dissolved in oil. Key gases: hydrogen (arcing), acetylene (severe arcing), ethylene (hot metal), CO/CO2 (cellulose degradation).
tests.moisture: Maximum 35 ppm for in-service oil up to 69kV. Moisture reduces dielectric strength.
tests.power_factor: Measures dielectric loss. Below 0.5% good. Above 1% = contaminated oil.

oil_analysis.frequency: Annual for critical transformers. Every 3 years for distribution transformers.
